title: Only accepted decisions may anchor automatic duplicate deletion
context: A real deterministic auto-review dry-run scheduled two human-confirmed benchmark proposals for deletion, each at 62% similarity to the other. Because proposed records are unresolved review candidates, reciprocal similarity supplied no evidence about which record should survive; applying the plan would delete both.
decision: Use one shared accepted-duplicate-anchor predicate across deterministic matching, harness candidate construction, and harness verdict validation. An anchor must be accepted, human-confirmed, and still live. Proposed decisions remain in the review queue even when they resemble or reciprocally name one another.
consequences: Automatic duplicate deletion cannot erase both sides of an unresolved proposal pair.
Harness output cannot promote an arbitrary proposal id into a deletion anchor.
Drafts that duplicate a finalized accepted decision continue to be eligible for deterministic deletion.
alternatives_rejected: Break ties between mutually similar proposals and keep one — similarity provides no authority to choose the survivor, so this would automate review judgment.
Fix only deterministic matching — a harness duplicate_of verdict could still name a proposal and recreate the same destructive behavior.
Disable duplicate deletion entirely — unnecessary; accepted decisions remain a stable and human-authorized comparison baseline.
